title: yucca harrimaniae subsp. neomexicana
intro: Yucca harrimaniae subsp. neomexicana, also known as New Mexican yucca, is a subspecies of the yucca plant that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of this plant come from its roots, which contain saponins and other compounds that have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of Yucca harrimaniae subsp. neomexicana include reducing inflammation, improving joint health, and supporting digestive health, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and wellness.
